business.headline: Govt allows states to double daily 5 kg LPG allocation for migrant workersIn an order issued on April 6, the government said the daily quantity of 5 kg free trade LPG (FTL) cylinders available for distribution to migrant labourers will be doubled, based on the average daily number of cylinders supplied on March 2 and 3, 2026.

Live: Supreme Court to take up long-pending Sabarimala verdict review shortlySabarimala SC Hearing Live Updates: A nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court will begin final hearings from April 7 on petitions concerning discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple, and the scope of religious freedom. The Constitution bench is led by Chief Justice Surya Kant. The case stems from a 2018 verdict, in which the top court, by a 4-1 majority, allowed entry of women aged 10 to 50 into the Sabarimala shrine, calling the ban unconstitutional. In 2019, a separate five-judge bench referred broader questions on women's entry and religious freedom across faiths to a larger bench for consideration. The Centre and the Kerala government have backed pleas for a review of the 2018 verdict. Stay with Indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Nutella scores a free ad in space, thanks to NASA livestream and perfect timingA jar of Nutella drifted into frame during NASA's Artemis II livestream, turning a zero-gravity moment into an unexpectedly cinematic "product placement" that left the internet amused.

Live: Iran adviser gives Trump '20 hours' to surrender amid Hormuz rowUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran, warning that "power plants and bridges" could be targeted if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening, saying "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one." Trump's abusive threat drew a sharp from Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who warned that Trump's "reckless moves" risk dragging the country into living hell and insisting "you won't gain anything through war crimes." Behind the scenes, Oman has held urgent talks with Iran to ensure smooth flow of transit through war-hit Hormuz, while Pakistan and Egypt have kept diplomatic channels open between Washington and Tehran. At the same time, violence has intensified elsewhere, with Israeli strikes in Beirut killing at least four people and Hezbollah launching retaliatory attacks, escalating the conflict further. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.
